rulings.Case Title: DIANE CONNELL VS. HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, June 10, 2013, Line 1. Defendant FERMA CORPORATION'S Motion to Compel Further Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The pro tem judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. COUNSEL TO APPEAR WITH PROPOSED ORDERS. COUNSEL SHOULD BE PREPARED TO DISCUSS, AMONG THE OTHER MATTERS RAISED IN THE PAPERS, THE CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE AND SPECIFICALLY WHETHER THE INFORMATION IN THE PRIVILEGE LOG IS SUFFICIENT TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF AMENDED CCP §2031.240(C)(1) EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2013. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIETER PFEIFER VS. RAINFOREST CAFE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, June 10, 2013, Line 2. Plaintiff DIETER PFEIFER'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE 5/28/13 OPPOSITION AND ANY ADDITIONAL OPPOSITION THAT MAY HAVE BEEN FILED BY DEFENDANT ON 6/6/13 (SEE LOCAL RULE 2.6(B)). BASED ON THE MOVING PAPERS ONLY, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS TO "GRANT THE MOTION EXCEPT AS TO SANCTIONS WHICH ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,750 AGAINST THE DEFENDANT ONLY (BECAUSE NO REPLY WAS FILED AND NO SPECIFIC REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST COUNSEL WAS INCLUDED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION (SEE BLUMENTHAL V. SUP. CT.. (1980) 103 CA3d 317, 320)). THE OBJECTIONS TO THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS ON THE GROUNDS OF VAGUENESS, AMBIGUITY, AND RELEVANCE ARE OVERRULED. FURTHER, DEFENDANT HAS NOT IDENTIFIED WITH PARTICULARITY THE DOCUMENTS BEING WITHHELD PURSUANT TO CLAIMS OF PRIVILEGE AS REQUIRED BY THE AMENDMENT TO CCP §2031.240(C)(1) EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2013." IF DEFENDANT WISHES TO BE HEARD FURTHER, ITS COUNSEL SHOULD CONTEST THE TENTATIVE WITH NOTICE TO ALL, PROVIDE ALL OPPOSITION PAPERS BY EMAIL TO MSCHREIBER@CWCLAW.COM BY 6 PM ON FRIDAY, 6/7/13, AND APPEAR AT THE HEARING. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY POWELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO CONSERVATION CORPS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Of Joint Submission Regarding Production Of Documents; Joint Submission
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, June 10, 2013, Line 3. Plaintiff SHIRLEY POWELL'S Motion of Joint Submission Regarding Production of Documents; Joint Submission Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. PARTIES TO APPEAR WITH PROPOSED ORDERS. PARTIES SHOULD BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS THE ADEQUACY OF NOTICE TO THE NON-PARTY WHOSE EMPLOYMENT RECORDS ARE SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFF AND THE ADEQUACY OF THE OPPORTUNITY FOR THE NON-PARTY TO OBJECT AS GAUGED AGAINST THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP §1985.6. FURTHER, PARTIES SHOULD BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS THE EXTENT TO WHICH ALTERNATIVE DISCOVERY IS AVAILABLE AND HAS BEEN PURSUED TO OBTAIN THE INFORMATION SOUGHT THROUGH PRODUCTION OF THE NON-PARTY EMPLOYEE RECORDS. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN SEGELKE VS. OGDEN CONTRACT INTERIORS, INC. A et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 10, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT OGDEN CONTRACT INTERIORS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained, without leave to amend. No opposition was filed. Plaintiff has not pled any facts to show a causal link between Defendants' alleged misrepresentations regarding the safety of his personal information and the industrial injury he suffered. Plaintiff has not alleged that he was employed by Defendants. Plaintiff has not alleged sufficient facts to establish any duty owed to him by Defendants. These allegations are conclusory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CONNIE H. SEBASTIAN VS. SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 10, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The hearing on the Demurrer to Complaint is continued to June 27, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. The reason for the continuance is that the Court still does not have a courtesy copy of the opposition brief that Plaintiffs filed in opposition to the demurrer. The Court needs a copy of Plaintiff's opposition brief in order to rule on this motion. Plaintiff must drop off a courtesy copy of its opposition brief directly to Department 302, the Law and Motion courtroom (not the Clerk's office). This is required under Local Rule 2.6B. A courtesy copy must be provided by June 20, 2013, by 4:00 p.m., with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. (In addition, the Court would appreciate receiving a courtesy copy of the opposition papers from counsel for Defendant, if possible.) Counsel for Defendant is instructed to provide notice of the continuation of the hearing on the demurrer, and an order that states the substance of this tentative ruling, and to bring proof of service of this order to the hearing on June 27, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 10, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTS ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, PAULA BUTLER, WILLIAM HUTTON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The Court overrules the demurrer and grants the motion to stay. Staying the action avoids the risk of inconsistent factual findings. For example, the issue regarding vicarious liability among the defendants should be resolved prior to an adjudication regarding indemnity rights. In addition, defendant is prejudiced by having to fight litigation on two-fronts, and this prejudice outweighs any showing of prejudice made by plaintiff insurance companies with regard to their ability to settle the underlying case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
